welcome:
CHAPTER 1

Welcome — This Changes Everything

Congratulations. If you're reading this, you've just gained access to one of the most comprehensive trade simulation and analysis tools ever built for TradingView.

But before we talk about settings and buttons, let's talk about why this matters to you.

Think about the hours you've already invested.

The late nights staring at charts. The endless manual backtesting — one model, one asset, one timeframe at a time — just to squeeze out a microscopic improvement in confidence. The trades you entered out of FOMO because you were terrified price would run without you, only to watch your position bleed red because the entry was poor. The losing streaks that made you question whether you ever had an edge at all.

That era is over.

The Infinite Outcome Trade Simulator detects every qualifying setup across five ICT-based models, simulates them with your exact rules, and hands you the irrefutable statistical truth about how your configurations actually perform. Not opinions. Not feelings. Numbers.

No longer sitting at the charts for hours backtesting one model across one asset in a single timeframe, hoping for a slightly improved view of what to expect.

No longer staring endlessly at the chart hoping your setup shows its hand.

No longer entering positions out of FOMO because "what if price runs without me?" — leading to poor entries and negative results.

No longer doubting your trading abilities after a bad run of losses.

No longer wondering what the odds are or whether price action is in your favour after entering a position.

Now you fine-tune your own trading model exactly to your liking.

Now you use the Insight Monitor to tweak, refine, and validate your setup with real statistics.

Now you know how your unique configuration has performed recently and over time — before you've even entered a trade.

Now you create alerts for your custom model and walk away from the chart. When the alert sounds, you open the charts, check the statistics, read the price action, and make your decision with full confidence.

🔥 KEY INSIGHT
Full accountability is now on you. You have the tool. You have the data. The only variable left is your discipline. Let's get you set up.
first-load:
CHAPTER 2

Adding the Indicator & First Load

Open any chart on TradingView, click the Indicators button (or press /), and search for "IOTSIM" or "Infinite Outcome Trade Simulator". Click to add it.

INSERT SCREENSHOT
TradingView indicator search showing IOTSIM in the results list

What You'll See on First Load

With default settings, the indicator scans the most recent 5,000 bars using all five models in both directions:

Detected Setups (blue labels/rays by default) — Pending limit orders waiting to be filled.

Active Longs (green labels by default) — Open trades tracked in real time.

Active Shorts (red labels by default) — Same, short direction.

Insight Monitor — On-chart stats table (default: bottom-right) with Total, Long, Short columns.

PD Array Visuals — FVG boxes, Order Blocks, Breaker Blocks, Wickless/Tailless rays, BOS/MSS labels, BSL/SSL levels, HTF IPDA lines.

HTF Scout — Optional Higher Timeframe FVG and OB tracking for trend alignment (off by default).

INSERT SCREENSHOT
Full chart showing detected (blue), active (green/red), PD Array boxes, and the Insight Monitor
⚠️ HEADS UP
If the chart looks cluttered on first load, don't panic. The Quick Switches section covers how to toggle each visual category on or off with a single click.
emoji-key:
CHAPTER 3

Emoji Key & Label Legend

The indicator uses emojis on trade labels for instant visual context.

Trade Status Emojis

EmojiNameMeaning
🎱DetectedEntry hasn't been hit yet
🔥ActiveEntry hit — tracked live
✅WinClosed in profit
❌LossClosed at a loss
⛔MissedTP reached before entry filled
⏰EOD CloseAuto-closed at end of day

Modifier & Context Emojis

EmojiNameMeaning
📐Dev CappedTarget adjusted by Min/Max Dev filter or rounding
💰Dollar CappedTarget capped by Dollar/Tick Hard Cap
⏲️KillzoneDetected during active ICT Killzone
⏱️MacroDetected during active ICT Macro window
🎛️TP ModifiedTarget Modifiers applied
🎚️SL ModifiedSL Modifiers applied
🛑Circuit BreakerDaily Circuit Breaker halted trade
❗Depth WarningFewer PD Arrays found than requested
🌐HTF AlignedSetup aligned with HTF Scout FVG/OB bias
💡 GOOD TO KNOW
Emojis can be toggled in Group 42 (Trade Labels & Rays). Killzone and Macro emojis are OFF by default — enable them when analysing session performance.
trade-label:
CHAPTER 4

Reading a Trade Label

Every trade has three labels: Entry, TP (Take Profit), and SL (Stop Loss). The Trade ID letter links all three.

Entry Label

ComponentExampleMeaning
DirectionLong / ShortSide of market
ModelFVG / OB / Disp / i-FVG / BBICT model that detected it
Trade IDA, B, … AA, ABUnique letter across all labels
Price21,450.25Calculated entry level
PnL$1,250.00Current or final value
(Hypo)(Hypo) $800Hypothetical PnL for missed trades

TP Label

ComponentExampleMeaning
Dev Multiplier2.5xDisplacement-lengths the target extends
Target TypePD Matrix(3)Method and depth
TP + IDTP AConfirms TP belongs to trade A

SL Label

ComponentExampleMeaning
SL MethodPD Matrix(2)How the stop was calculated
SL + IDAConfirms SL belongs to trade A
🎯 PRO TIP
The Trade ID letter is your anchor. Entry A, TP A, and SL A all belong to the same trade. Use this to visually trace any setup.

account-risk:
CHAPTER 6

Account & Risk Setup (Group 4)

Getting Group 4 right is critical. Wrong numbers = wrong dollar figures on the Insight Monitor.

📊 FUTURES (NQ, ES, YM, CL, GC)
PnL = Price Move × Point Value × Contracts
Auto-detected. Keep Margin 1.0, Leverage 1.0. Only change Contracts.
🪙 CRYPTO, FOREX & EQUITIES
PnL = Price Move × (Margin × Leverage / Entry) × Contracts
Auto-Detect OFF. Set Margin, Leverage, Contracts manually.

Step-by-Step

1
Decide total position size in dollars (e.g. $10,000).
2
Your own capital = Margin (e.g. $1,000).
3
Exchange leverage = Leverage (e.g. 10x).
4
Sanity check: Margin × Leverage = Buying Power.
5
Contracts = units traded (0.15 BTC, 100 shares, etc.).

Real-World Examples

AssetMarginLeverageContractsBuying Power
BTC (spot)$10,0001x0.15$10,000
BTC (10x perp)$1,00010x0.15$10,000
SOL (5x perp)$2,0005x50$10,000
PEPE (spot)$5001x25,000,000$500
AAPL (cash)$10,0001x100$10,000
NQ Futures$1.001x1Auto (Point Value)
ES Futures$1.001x2Auto (Point Value)
⚠️ HEADS UP
For futures, leave Margin at 1.0 and Leverage at 1.0. Auto-Detect handles everything. Only change Contracts.

Ticks Mode

Alternative: set Global Value to "Ticks" for raw price movement instead of dollars. Common for intraday index futures traders.
presets:
CHAPTER 7

Recommended Presets by Asset Class

Starting points to refine using the Insight Monitor. Every market is different — the tool helps you discover what works best for you.

7.1 Index Futures (NQ, ES, YM)

Highly liquid, predictable sessions. ICT Killzones align perfectly. Timeframes: 1m–5m.

INSERT SCREENSHOT
Index Futures chart with IOTSIM showing trade setups

Account & Risk (Group 4)

SettingRecommendedWhy
Global ValueDollars or TicksDollars for standard; Ticks for raw movement
Auto-DetectONReads $20/pt NQ, $50/pt ES, $5/pt YM
Contracts1Your actual count

Global Trade Mgmt (Group 5)

SettingRecommendedWhy
EOD Auto-CloseONEssential for day trading
IPDA TimeframeDDaily standard
Swing Strength5Dominant intraday swings
HTF Scout TF601H provides solid intraday bias

Core Setup (Groups 6–11)

SettingRecommendedWhy
ModelsAll 5 enabledWide net — narrow via Leaderboard
Entry Level0.34Optimal Trade Entry zone
SL MethodPD Array MatrixStructural SL anchor
SL Depth22nd PD Array behind entry

Killzones (Group 29)

SettingRecommendedWhy
Enable KillzonesONHigh-probability ICT sessions
LondonON02:00–05:00 EST
NY AMON07:00–10:00 EST
🎯 PRO TIP
Alternative SL approach: try "Adjustable R" with R:R of 0.3 for a tighter, fixed-ratio stop. Compare on the Insight Monitor.

7.2 Commodities (CL, GC)

Wider ranges, stronger momentum. Deeper retracements. Timeframes: 1m–5m.

INSERT SCREENSHOT
Commodities chart with IOTSIM showing trade setups

Key Differences

SettingRecommendedWhy
Entry Level0.382Commodities retrace deeper
SL Depth2–3Wider stops for volatility
SL BufferONPrevents premature stop-outs
EOD Auto-CloseONIntraday CL/GC
🎯 PRO TIP
Try Entry Level at 0.5 (equilibrium) for Gold. GC often respects the 50% level more cleanly than OTE.

7.3 Crypto (BTC, ETH, SOL)

24/7 markets, no session close. Killzones still apply. Timeframes: 5m–15m.

EOD Auto-CloseOFFNo session boundaries
Entry Level0.5Crypto retraces to equilibrium

7.4 Equities (AAPL, SPY)

Large-cap stocks with good volume. Timeframes: 1m–15m.

monitor:
CHAPTER 8

The Insight Monitor

Your command centre. Every closed trade processed, every stat calculated, the statistical truth at your fingertips.

Monitor Pages

📊Full Stats
Complete breakdown: Total, Long, Short columns. How to use it: Primary analysis page. Compare directions, find what drives profitability.
📋Overview
Condensed 4-row snapshot: WR, Net PnL, Peak DD, counts. How to use it: Quick health check. Swap for a cleaner chart.
📅Day Analysis
WR, PnL, Profit Factor, best hour per day of week. How to use it: Find best/worst days. Adjust exposure.
🕐Hour Analysis
WR, PnL, Profit Factor, top-performing weekday per hour (EST). How to use it: Find profitable hours. Focus Killzones.
📜Recent Trades
Scrollable list: ID, model, direction, PnL. How to use it: Quick reference for recent outcomes.
🏆Model Leaderboard
Ranks all 10 model/direction combos by PF and PnL. How to use it: Most powerful page. For intraday, LOWER lookback to 200–500 bars to see what works RIGHT NOW.
INSERT SCREENSHOT
Side-by-side: Full Stats page and Model Leaderboard page
stat-rows:
CHAPTER 9

Choosing Your Stat Rows (Group 32)

Recommended rows with context on how to use them.

RowWhy You Want It
Detected/ClosedHow many found vs completed — high ratio = most trigger
Win RateMost fundamental. >50% + decent R:R = edge
Win/Loss StreaksUnderstand variance. Max loss streak ≠ broken setup
Net/Avg Real $Bottom line. The number that pays the bills
Profit FactorGross profit ÷ loss. >1.0 profitable, >2.0 excellent
Max/Avg Unr. $Unrealised profit before close. Much higher than Net? Use partials
Max/Avg DDWorst drawdown per trade. Exceeds tolerance? Tighten stops
Max/Avg SL Unr.$Profit before SL hit. Goldmine — if +$500 before SL, set TP1 there
TP1 Hits & TotalHow often TP1 hit + value. Compare with Net PnL for dependency
SL Hits% ending at SL. High + low SL unrealised = stops too tight
Peak Max RealisedEquity high-water mark. Should trend up if edge exists
Peak Max DDWorst peak-to-trough. Most important risk metric
💡 GOOD TO KNOW
Enable or disable any row without affecting the simulation. Stats are always computed — checkboxes only control visibility.

Visual Customisation (Group 31)

SettingRecommendedWhy
PositionBottom RightKeeps it away from price action
SizeTiny or SmallTiny fits most screens
BackgroundBlack (10% transp)See candles behind it
Positive StatGreen #00E676Winning metrics
Negative StatRed #FF5252Losing metrics
WarningOrange #FF7300Missed entries, SL stats
NeutralWhiteInformational rows
🎯 PRO TIP
Once fine-tuned, hide the Monitor (Show Trade Insights OFF in Group 1). Bring it back anytime. The simulation runs regardless.
quick-switches:
CHAPTER 10

Controlling Chart Clutter: Quick Switches

Group 2 is your visual command panel. Colours mentioned are defaults — fully customisable in Group 42.

SwitchDefaultWhat It Controls
Show DetectedONPending setups (blue labels/rays by default)
Show ActiveONOpen trades (green longs, red shorts by default)
Show All ClosedOFFEvery completed trade — dense, for review
Show MissedOFFTP hit before entry (⛔ missed)
Show WinsOFFProfitable trades only — pattern study
Show LossesOFFLosing trades only — SL analysis
Show 2nd ChanceOFFSwept then re-entered setups
Show PartialsOFFTP1/TP2/TP3 partial lines and labels
🎯 PRO TIP
For active trading, keep only Detected + Active ON. Enable Wins/Losses for studying patterns.
next-steps:
CHAPTER 11

Next Steps — Your First Week

1
Let it accumulate data. 200–500+ closed trades before strong conclusions. On 5m, can happen in a single day.
2
Study the Insight Monitor. Win Rate, Profit Factor, Peak Max DD = three primary health indicators. WR >50% + PF >1.5 = edge.
3
Use the Model Leaderboard. See which model/direction combos carry profitability. Disable underperformers in Group 6 / 17.
4
Rotate through pages. Day Analysis for best/worst days. Hour Analysis for profitable hours. Use the Killzone Win Rate row to see session edge.
5
Adjust the Lookback Period. High lookback (3,000–5,000) = broad baseline. But price goes through contraction/expansion, trend/reversal. For intraday: start high for baseline, then LOWER to 200–500 bars to align with current conditions. That's where real edge lives.
6
Set up alerts. Group 45: Detected, Entry Hit, TP1 Hit, TSL Update. Walk away. Alert fires → check Monitor → read PA → decide.
7
Read the companion guides. Walkthrough Guide for section depth. Complete User Manual for every single setting.
🔥 KEY INSIGHT
The tool doesn't trade for you. It gives you the information to trade with conviction. The setups are yours. The stats are yours. The edge is yours. Now go find it.
